description.content: The Organization Social Actions Notifications API enables your app to notify members about activity on their LinkedIn Company Pages. Use this API to build real-time streams for activity on LinkedIn Company Pages.

item.name: Use Cases > Social Actions Notifications > Organization Social Actions Notifications - Pull Workflow

description.content: Notifications for the authenticated member’s organizations can be queried using a criteria finder. Notifications are retained and available to pull for 60 days.

item.name: LinkedIn 2. Create a Share with a Company Mention

request.name: LinkedIn 2. Create a Share with a Company Mention

description.content:
Mention a company name by using the annotations. [Learn more](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/linkedin/marketing/integrations/community-management/shares/share-api?tabs=http#share-text-and-mentions) by viewing our public documentation.

This example uses the LinkedIn organization.

request.name: LinkedIn 3. Retrieve Notifications for the Authenticated Member's Organization

item.name: Use Cases > Social Actions Notifications > Organization Social Actions Notifications - Push Workflow

description.content: Registered webhook URLs will receive notifications from LinkedIn for subscribed events.

[Create a subscription](https://docs.microsoft.com/linkedin/marketing/integrations/community-management/organizations/organization-social-action-notifications?tabs=http#creating-and-updating-subscriptions) record to include the webhook URL where your app will receive notifications. The subscription request will only succeed if the subscribing member is an administrator of the organization being subscribed to.

Retrieve all the subscriptions details such as webhook url and expiration for the logged in user. [Learn more](https://docs.microsoft.com/linkedin/marketing/integrations/community-management/organizations/organization-social-action-notifications?tabs=http#retrieve-subscriptions-for-an-authenticated-member) by viewing our online documentation.

[Remove a subscription](https://docs.microsoft.com/linkedin/marketing/integrations/community-management/organizations/organization-social-action-notifications?tabs=http#remove-a-subscription) for a your developer application and Organization.
This API gets the statistics for Organization, Page and for Share.

item.name: Use Cases > Statistics APIs > Organization Follower Statistics

description.content: This API describes usage of Organization Follower Statistics. Fore more details, check [here](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/linkedin/marketing/integrations/community-management/organizations/follower-statistics?tabs=http&view=li-lms-unversioned).

item.name: Use Cases > Statistics APIs > Organization Page Statistics

description.content: This API describes usage of Organization Page Statistics. Fore more details, check [here](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/linkedin/marketing/integrations/community-management/organizations/page-statistics?tabs=http&view=li-lms-unversioned).

item.name: Use Cases > Statistics APIs > Organization Share Statistics

description.content: This API describes usage of Organization Share Statistics. Fore more details, check [here](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/linkedin/marketing/integrations/community-management/organizations/share-statistics?tabs=http).

**You must apply for access to the Marketing Developer Platform in order to use these APIs. See the** [**Developer Portal**](https://developer.linkedin.com/product-catalog/marketing/page-management) **on how to apply.**

This collection aims to focus on different Community Management use cases, for example, social action notifications, and should be used with the **community-management-env-versioning** environment.

\--All requests for the Community Management API collection are in the Restli 2.0 format--

AuthorizationOAuth 2.0
